text: In this video, we will be describing the procedure for laparoscopic removal of a large para-ovarian cyst.
The surgeon makes an initial incision at the mesosalpinx region using a laparoscopic shearer, extending the incision to allow for the intact removal of the large para-ovarian cyst.
The surgeon widens the incision at the mesosalpincheal region, applying gentle traction and counter traction to detach and remove the large para-ovarian cyst from its attachments.
The surgeon applies gentle traction to deliver the entire para-ovarian cyst from its attachment, ensuring it is removed intact regardless of its size.
The para-ovarian cyst is visibly liberated from the mesosalpines, remaining intact after careful dissection.
The surgeon ruptures the para-ovarian cyst, aspirates the fluid, and removes the cyst wall from the peritoneal cavity using laparoscopic instruments.
